patches.title: Let non-4:3 display modes pass enumeration
patches.purpose: Neutralize one non-4:3 mode rejection gate in display mode enumeration.
preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Patch is applied to BEA.exe-compatible retail layout.
side_effects: Enables non-4:3 candidates in mode enumeration path.
Does not by itself force startup window mode.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original byte at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched.

patches.title: Prefer windowed startup (when windowed-capable)
patches.purpose: Force startup path windowed-decision flag true on windowed-capable initialization flow.
preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Windowed-capable path is available on target runtime setup.
side_effects: Biases startup toward windowed mode.
Some environments may still require wrapper/driver companion settings.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original bytes at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched.

patches.title: Default GEFORCE_FX_POWER tweak on
patches.purpose: Change GEFORCE_FX_POWER tweak registration default from 0 to 1 so extra graphics options are available without cardid.txt vendor/device matches.
preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Patch is applied to BEA.exe-compatible retail layout.
side_effects: Extra graphics feature gate defaults to enabled at startup.
cardid.txt can still override tweak values if matching vendor/device rules apply.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original bytes at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched.

patches.title: Ignore cardid.txt vendor/device tweak overrides
patches.purpose: Bypass the startup call that loads and applies cardid.txt tweak overrides, so BEA.exe defaults are used directly.
preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Patch is applied to BEA.exe-compatible retail layout.
side_effects: cardid.txt vendor/device/version tweak overrides are not applied.
Intended code-path effect is for BEA.exe default tweak values to be used; visible graphics effect and rendering correctness remain separate runtime proof.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original bytes at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched.

patches.title: Install PATCHED version-overlay marker pointer

preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Patch is applied to BEA.exe-compatible retail layout.
side_effects: Version overlay render uses cave-hosted format string.
Used together with version cave string payload patch.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original bytes at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched.

patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original bytes at file offset.

patches.title: Frontend clear-screen dark red preset
patches.purpose: Change the DirectX frontend RenderStart clear-screen color immediate from source-backed dark blue to a dark red preset.
preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Patch is applied to BEA.exe-compatible retail layout.
Use as an opt-in frontend color mod; select only one frontend clear-screen color preset at a time.
side_effects: Changes the CDXFrontEnd RenderStart clear-screen color immediate only.
Does not replace textures, menu art, fonts, HUD colors, or gameplay rendering colors.
Runtime screenshots remain the visual proof gate for this specific color preset.

patches.title: Goodies gallery display flag override
patches.purpose: Force the CFEPGoodies Process MALLOY-derived display flag path true in the copied executable while preserving the stack for the skipped IsCheatActive(0) call; bounded safe-copy comparison shows the Goodies wall display state changes, while broader selection/model/FMV proof remains separate.
preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Patch is applied to BEA.exe-compatible retail layout.
Use as an opt-in Goodies wall display mod; broader Goodies selection, model-viewer, FMV, and every-entry behavior remain separate proof.
side_effects: Forces only the MALLOY-derived Goodies display flag path in CFEPGoodies Process.
Does not patch save files, award goodies permanently, force the lat\xEAte/developer Goodies override, or change the career buffer.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original bytes at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched.

patches.title: Bypass one startup fullscreen toggle check
patches.purpose: Skip one startup fullscreen-toggle gate when stable patches are insufficient on a target setup.
preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Use only after stable patches are verified and startup still forces fullscreen.
side_effects: Alters one startup decision branch only; not a universal fullscreen fix.
Runtime effect can vary by environment and wrapper stack.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original bytes at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched.

patches.title: Experimental: O scan for default pause initializer
patches.purpose: Change the copied executable's default single-binding initializer for BUTTON_PAUSE from DirectInput scan 0x01 to DirectInput scan 0x18, the O key candidate from the source PCController mapping; accepted exact-PID copied-runtime CDB proof covers ordered same-window O-query, BUTTON_PAUSE dispatch, and pause/unpause evidence in one bounded free-camera context.
preconditions: Target byte at offset matches expected original or already-patched byte.
Patch is applied to BEA.exe-compatible retail layout.
Use only as an experimental copied-executable pause-key row; broader normal gameplay pause/menu safety and control-feel proof remain separate gates.
side_effects: Changes only the retail default single-binding initializer row for button 0x38 in the copied executable.
Does not edit defaultoptions.bea, save files, controller configuration entries, or the installed Steam executable.

Accepted proof covers ordered same-window copied-profile free-camera pause/unpause evidence; full debugger call-chain causality, normal gameplay pause-menu UX, broad pause/menu safety, long-session safety, and control-feel behavior remain separate proof.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original byte at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched; runtime proof checker must show copied byte 0x18, live pause row keyArg 0x18, exact safe-copy PID/path binding, and ordered same-window O-query -> BUTTON_PAUSE dispatch -> pause/unpause evidence in the bounded context.

patches.title: Experimental: bypass Aurore free-camera gate
patches.purpose: NOP the retail-only Aurore inactive branch in CGame::ReceiveButtonAction so the existing BUTTON_TOGGLE_FREE_CAMERA case can fall through to its free-camera toggle path; accepted copied-runtime CDB proof covers scoped F toggle on/off camera-pointer transitions.
preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Patch is applied to BEA.exe-compatible retail layout.
Use as an experimental copied-executable debug camera mod; broader camera movement/control proof remains a separate behavior gate.
side_effects: Bypasses only the Aurore cheat gate in the debug button free-camera case.
Does not prove all camera controls, gameplay safety, pause/menu behavior, or free-camera rendering at runtime.
Can expose debug-camera behavior during gameplay if the corresponding debug button is reachable.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original bytes at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched.

patches.title: Experimental companion: free-camera Q-forward remap cave
patches.expected_original_bytes: CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC CC
patches.purpose: Install a copied-executable code-cave body that rewrites free-camera handler button 31 to camera-forward button 38 before continuing the original CControllableCamera::ReceiveButtonAction prologue.
preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Patch is applied to BEA.exe-compatible retail layout.
Use only as the hidden companion payload for the free-camera Q-forward hook.
side_effects: Installs code in an unused retail .text CC run.
Does not run unless the companion hook redirects CControllableCamera::ReceiveButtonAction into this cave.
One Q-forward copied-runtime CDB movement path is proven by the companion hook artifact; broader control feel and visible behavior remain separate proof.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original bytes at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched.

patches.title: Experimental: Q-forward free-camera hook
patches.purpose: Redirect the copied executable's CControllableCamera::ReceiveButtonAction entry through a cave that maps Q-bound button 31 to the camera-forward button value 38 before the original camera-control switch.
preconditions: Target bytes at offset match expected original or already-patched bytes.
Patch is applied to BEA.exe-compatible retail layout.
Use only with the copied-profile safe runtime path and free-camera gate bypass.
Accepted copied-runtime CDB proof shows Q-window cave hits, camera-handler rows, and camera-position deltas before any broader control claim.
side_effects: Changes only the copied executable's free-camera control handler entry path.
Maps button 31 to camera-forward only while execution is in CControllableCamera::ReceiveButtonAction.
Does not prove control feel, joystick/analog behavior, gameplay safety, pause/menu behavior, or rebuild parity.
patches.rollback_strategy: Restore BEA.exe.original.backup or rewrite original bytes at file offset.
patches.verification_probe: Verify selected patch state and ensure offset reports ready/already patched.

Use only as the hidden companion payload for the free-camera Q-backward hook.

Restore verifies patched bytes before restoring the original CC padding.
This row is not selectable by itself; it is added only as a dependency of free_camera_keyboard_backward_q_hook.

patches.title: Experimental: Q-backward free-camera hook

Apply to a copied executable only; never patch the installed Steam executable in place.
Use as one bounded reverse-movement proof path only. It conflicts with the Q-forward, Q-strafe-left, and Q-strafe-right remaps because these rows patch the same hook and cave bytes.
postconditions: Only the copied executable bytes at 0x01A980 are changed by this visible row; its hidden companion owns 0x1A3A15.
Restore verifies patched bytes before restoring the original CControllableCamera::ReceiveButtonAction prologue bytes.
Runtime proof must show scoped Q input reaching the hook/cave path and producing CDB-observed free-camera position deltas before any claim broadens beyond this one path.
